source_pr.title: *: support caching coprocessor responses
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

This PR adds support for the [Coprocessor Cache](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/9336) protocol.

### What is changed and how it works?

This PR adds a cache layer when sending requests and receiving requests.

This PR does not have correctness issues, however some trivial parts will be improved in future PRs:

- TODO: EXPLAIN ANALYZE should not affect cache.
- TODO: EXPLAIN ANALYZE should display real execution time instead of cached time.
- TODO: Add detailed metrics.
- TODO: Detect non-deterministic push down functions (currently they are not pushed down so it is still correct).
- TODO: Specifying old snapshot time should not affect cache.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test
 - Integration test
 - Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

symptoms: + // Changed from 10ms to 50ms to resist from unstable CI environment.
failure_signature: + // Changed from 10ms to 50ms to resist from unstable CI environment.
root_cause_explanation: The coprocessor cache (ristretto) updates asynchronously; immediately reading after a `Set` may not observe the value yet. A very short sleep (10ms) was insufficient under slower CI, causing timing-dependent failures. The fix increases the wait (`time.Sleep`) to give the async cache time to settle before asserting.
fix_pattern: Increase the wait for async cache visibility (e.g., `time.Sleep(10ms → 50ms)`) before assertions; prefer an Eventually/polling approach when feasible.

evidence: evidence: + // Changed from 10ms to 50ms to resist from unstable CI environment.
